# NTLSN — National Teaching and Learning Support Network

> A free, open commons connecting Australia's higher-education teaching and learning sector: every conference, showcase, workshop and professional-development opportunity across all 42 universities, plus a rescued 31-year archive of nationally funded teaching scholarship. No logins, no paywalls. Licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Founded by A/Prof Seb Dianati (University of Southern Queensland).

## Core
- [NTLSN home](https://www.ntlsn.com): the full platform — interactive sector map, events calendar, resources, and recognition tools across all 42 Australian universities.
- [Sector Archive](https://www.ntlsn.com/#ntlsn-archive): 1,431 ALTC- and OLT-funded learning and teaching works, 1994–2025. NTLSN indexes titles and authors; full text lives at the Australian Government Learning & Teaching Repository.
- [Best Practice guides](https://www.ntlsn.com/#ntlsn-bestpractice): 80 curated Good Practice Reports and guides, 2006–2024 (academic integrity, work-integrated learning, assessment, curriculum design, student transition).

## Free in-browser tools
- [GenAI Assignment Redesign](https://www.ntlsn.com/assignment-redesign.html): paste an assignment brief and see where a generative AI could do the work for a student, with a vulnerability score and five evidence-based redesign moves (personal, process, applied, interactive, creative) to make it authentically human. In-browser, nothing stored.
- [AI Prompts for Teaching & Learning](https://www.ntlsn.com/teaching-ai-prompts.html): a curated, copy-paste library of AI prompts for the real work of teaching and SoTL — assessment design, constructive alignment, feedback, reflective practice, accessibility, curriculum and teaching portfolios — each with a brief note on why it works. Use with any AI assistant; nothing stored.
- [Learn from the Sector](https://www.ntlsn.com/learning-modules.html): self-paced micro-modules that turn the sector’s talks into structured learning — watch on a theme, answer concept-check questions, read the key ideas, reflect, and track completion. Each module also exports as a SCORM 1.2 package to upload into any LMS (Moodle, Canvas, Blackboard), reporting completion and quiz score to the gradebook. In-browser; progress stays on the device.
- [Teaching & Learning Leadership Pathway](https://www.ntlsn.com/leadership.html): a free self-assessment for academics moving into teaching & learning leadership — rate yourself across six evidence-based leadership capabilities (grounded in Scott, Coates & Anderson's 2008 ALTC study, *Learning Leaders in Times of Change*) and get a development pathway matched to NTLSN tools and the sector's leadership programs (WATTLE, Advance HE Aurora, HERDSA). In-browser, nothing stored.
- [Missed it? Catch up](https://www.ntlsn.com/missed-it.html): a catch-up hub for recently-passed sector events — NTLSN points you to the recordings, programs and slides organisers post after workshops, webinars and conferences. Drawn from NTLSN's open events data; independent of the organisers; links go to the host.
- [School T&L Leadership Check](https://www.ntlsn.com/school-leadership.html): a maturity self-assessment for Heads of School, Associate Deans (Education) and T&L leads — rate your school's collective teaching & learning leadership and culture across seven dimensions (shared vision, distributed leadership, teaching culture & recognition, educator development, evidence-informed improvement, assessment & GenAI integrity, student partnership & inclusion) and get a prioritised plan matched to NTLSN tools. In-browser, nothing stored.
- [Casual Teaching Starter Kit](https://www.ntlsn.com/casual-teaching.html): a free, practical starter kit for casual and sessional academics — seven essentials with checklists (before your first class, running a tutorial, marking fairly & consistently, feedback, academic integrity & GenAI, your rights/time/wellbeing including being paid for marking time, and where it can grow). Progress saves on-device; honest that it is not industrial or legal advice.
- [Your Academic Year](https://www.ntlsn.com/academic-year.html): a personalised teaching-year planner — set your semester start date and get your year as a rhythm (prep, teaching, the mid-semester breather, the marking crunch, recovery) with what to do and which NTLSN tool to reach for at each phase, plus the Universities Australia common vacation weeks. Phase dates are approximate; confirm exact dates with your university. In-browser, nothing stored.
- [The NTLSN Teaching Diagnostic](https://www.ntlsn.com/diagnostic.html): one comprehensive self-review that ties the whole toolkit together — rate a unit, course or whole program across alignment, assessment, accessibility, partnership, quality, SoTL and recognition, and get a prioritised action plan routing to the right tool for each gap. In-browser, no login.
- [Live SoTL Research Search](https://www.ntlsn.com/research-search.html): search current teaching & learning research live from OpenAlex (250M+ open scholarly works), with open-access links surfaced — the living companion to the 1,431-work historical archive.
- [Find Your SoTL Conference](https://www.ntlsn.com/conference-finder.html): a three-question wizard — interest, format, timing — that matches you to the right Australian teaching & learning event from the live calendar (e.g. a student-partnership interest routes you to the Students as Partners Roundtable). In-browser, no login.
- [Open-Access Finder](https://www.ntlsn.com/oa-finder.html): paste a DOI, several DOIs, or a whole reference list and get the free, legal open-access version of each paper, colour-coded by openness (diamond / gold / green / hybrid / closed). Resolved live via OpenAlex; nothing leaves the browser.
- [Where to Publish Your SoTL](https://www.ntlsn.com/journal-finder.html): search the Directory of Open Access Journals (DOAJ) by topic to find the open-access journal for your teaching scholarship — filtered to the ones that are free to publish in (no APC / diamond OA), with peer-review type, licence and author guidelines. In-browser, no login.
- [ATEC · TEQSA Curriculum QA](https://www.ntlsn.com/atec-curriculum-qa.html): self-check a course's outcomes against its AQF level (Bloom's verbs), ATEC mission-compact alignment (widening participation, VET→HE), and the GenAI-resilience of its assessments. Indicative, in-browser.
- [Course Quality Self-Check](https://www.ntlsn.com/selfcheck.html): rate a course against the TELAS standards and UDL guidelines; produces a readiness profile.
- [TEQSA GenAI Readiness](https://www.ntlsn.com/teqsa-readiness.html): self-assess a course or program against TEQSA generative-AI guidance.
- [Teaching Recognition Passport](https://www.ntlsn.com/trp.html): a portable, self-asserted record of teaching contribution.
- [Reflective Coach](https://www.ntlsn.com/reflective-coach.html): a Socratic scaffold across established reflective-practice models.
- [Students-as-Partners Certificate](https://www.ntlsn.com/cert.html): generate a printable student-staff partnership certificate as portfolio evidence.

## What the sector can find here
- Teaching and learning events, conferences, grants and fellowships, frameworks and policy documents, career pathways, and curated sector and international SoTL talks, all aggregated across Australian higher education.

## Open data & API
- [Hosted MCP server](https://www.ntlsn.com/.netlify/functions/mcp): a live Model Context Protocol endpoint over HTTP — point any MCP-compatible AI client at this URL to query NTLSN sector data (events, the 1,431-work archive, universities). No setup, no key.
- [Open Data & API](https://www.ntlsn.com/developers.html): every dataset as open, CORS-enabled JSON — events, the 1,431-work archive, 80 best-practice guides, frameworks, and the 42-university directory. Free to query, embed via a one-line widget, or wire into any AI assistant with a copy-paste MCP server. No key, no logins.

## About and licensing
- NTLSN is a free, open Open Educational Practice commons. Paid services exist only to sustain the commons and never gate it. Curation and original benchmarking are licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0; linked third-party works remain the property of their authors and institutions.
- NTLSN does not claim endorsement by the universities, peak bodies, or regulators it catalogues.
- [Stream your symposium](https://www.ntlsn.com/symposium-streaming.html): pilot — connect your university's own Zoom (official OAuth) and NTLSN runs symposium registration, the live embed and the replay; sign in with ORCID. In design with founding universities.
- [Crash Courses hub — the commons distilled](https://www.ntlsn.com/crash-courses.html)
- [Authentic Assessment crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/assessment-crash-course.html)
- [Feedback That Works crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/feedback-crash-course.html)
- [Inclusive & Accessible Teaching crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/inclusive-teaching-crash-course.html)
- [First-Year Success & Belonging crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/first-year-crash-course.html)
- [Designing Your Course crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/curriculum-crash-course.html)
- [Academic Integrity crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/integrity-crash-course.html)
- [Work-Integrated Learning crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/wil-crash-course.html)
- [Large-Class Teaching crash course](https://www.ntlsn.com/large-class-crash-course.html)
